sarga.title: Bharata's joy on locating the hermitage of Sri Rama.

shlokas.original:
निवेश्य सेनां तु विभुः पद्भ्यां पादवतां वरः।
अभिगन्तुं स काकुत्थ्समियेष गुरुवर्तकम्।।2.98.1।।
shlokas.translation: On encamping the army, lord Bharata, the best among men, wished to approach on  foot Rama who was devoted to his father.

shlokas.original:
निविष्टमात्रे सैन्ये तु यथोद्देशं विनीतवत्।
भरतो भ्रातरं वाक्यं शत्रुघ्नमिदमब्रवीत्।।2.98.2।।
shlokas.translation: After the army encamped in the assigned places, Bharata said to his brother Satrughna thus:

shlokas.original:
क्षिप्रं वनमिदं सौम्य नरसङ्घै स्समन्ततः।
लुब्धैश्च सहितैरेभि स्त्वमन्वेषितुमर्हसि।।2.98.3।।
shlokas.translation: O dear Satrughna, you should explore this forest quickly along with groups of our  people and  hunters as well.

shlokas.original:
गुहो ज्ञातिसहस्रेण शरचापासिधारिणा।
समन्वेषतु काकुत्स्थमस्मिन् परिवृतस्स्वयम्।।2.98.4।।
shlokas.translation: Let Guha, surrounded by a thousand kinsmen and armed with bows, arrows and swords look for Rama.

shlokas.original:
अमात्यै स्सह पौरैश्च गुरुभिश्च द्विजातिभिः।
वनं सर्वं चरिष्यामि पद्भ्यां परिवृत स्स्वयम्।।2.98.5।।
shlokas.translation: 'I myself shall go on foot surrounded by ministers, citizens, preceptors and brahmins and search the entire forest.

shlokas.original:
यावन्न रामं द्रक्ष्यामि लक्ष्मणं वा महाबलम्।
वैदेहीं च महाभागां न मे शान्तिर्भविष्यति।।2.98.6।।
shlokas.translation: 'I shall not rest in peace until I see Rama, powerful Lakshmana and illustrious princess of Videha (Sita).

shlokas.original:
यावन्न चन्द्रसङ्काशं द्रक्ष्यामि शुभमाननम्। 
भ्रातुः पद्मपलाशाक्षं न मे शान्तिर्भविष्यति।।2.98.7।।
shlokas.translation: I shall not attain peace until I see my brother, Rama whose auspicious countenance looks radiant as the Moon and whose eyes as lotuspetals.

shlokas.original:
यावन्न चरणौ भ्रातुः पार्थिवव्यञ्जनान्वितौ।
शिरसा धारयिष्यामि न मे शान्तिर्भविष्यति।।2.98.8।।
shlokas.translation: Peace shall not come to me until I hold on my head the feet of my brother, the insignia of royalty.

shlokas.original:
यावन्न राज्ये राज्यार्हः पितृपैतामहे स्थितः।
अभिषेकजलक्लिन्नो न मे शान्तिर्भविष्यति।।2.98.9।।
shlokas.translation: I shall not attain peace until Rama, who is worthy of this kingdom, is sprinkled with the holy water of coronation and assumes the ancestral kingdom.

shlokas.original:
सिद्धार्थः खलु सौमित्रिर्यश्चन्द्रविमलोपमम्।
मुखं पश्यति रामस्य राजीवाक्षं महाद्युति।।2.98.10।।
shlokas.translation: Lakshmana, one who beholds the highly effulgent countenance of Rama, resembling the spotless Moon and whose eyes resemble red lotuses, is blessed indeed

shlokas.original:
कृतकृत्या महाभागा वैदेही जनकात्मजा।
भर्तारं सागरान्तायाः पृथिव्या यानुगच्छति।।2.98.11।।
shlokas.translation: Sita, the illustrious daughter of Janaka and princess of Videha, has accomplished her purpose by following Rama, the lord of this earth bounded by the ocean.

shlokas.original:
सुभगश्चित्रकूटोऽसौ गिरिराजोपमो गिरिः।
यस्मिन्वसति काकुत्स्थः कुबेर इव नन्दने।।2.98.12।।
shlokas.translation: Like Kubera in the Nandana garden, Rama lives here on mount Chitrakuta which is indeed blessed like the Himalaya, king of the mountains.

shlokas.original:
कृतकार्यमिदं दुर्गं वनं व्यालनिषेवितम्।
यदध्यास्ते महातेजा राम श्शस्त्रभृतां वरः।।2.98.13।।
shlokas.translation: Fortunate is this inaccessible forest which is inhabited by wild animals to have become the dwelling place of the most radiant Rama, the foremost among the wielders of weapons.

shlokas.original:
एवमुक्त्वा महातेजा भरतः पुरषर्षभः।
पद्भ्यामेव महाबाहुः प्रविवेश महाद्वनम्।।2.98.14।।
shlokas.translation: Having said thus, the best of men, mightyarmed and highly energetic Bharata entered the great forest on foot.

shlokas.original:
स तानि द्रुमजालानि जातानि गिरिसानुषु।
पुष्पिताग्राणि मध्येन जगाम वदतां वरः।।2.98.15।।
shlokas.translation: Bharata, the best among the eloquent, made his way amidst multitude of trees grown on mountain slopes with their tops in full bloom.

shlokas.original:
स गिरेश्चित्रकूटस्य सालमासाद्य पुष्पितम्।
रामाश्रमगतस्याग्नेर्ददर्श ध्वजमुच्छ्रितम्।।2.98.16।।
shlokas.translation: Having reached a flowering sala tree atop Chitrakuta mountain, he beheld a lofty banner of smoke rising from the fire burning in Rama's hermitage.

shlokas.original:
तं दृष्ट्वा भरत श्रीमान्मुमोद सह बान्धवः।
अत्र राम इति ज्ञात्वा गतः पारमिवाम्भसः।।2.98.17।।
shlokas.translation: On seeing the smoke, majestic Bharata in the company of relatives concluded that Rama was staying there and rejoiced like a man who had reached the other bank of the river.

shlokas.original:
स चित्रकूटे तु गिरौ निशम्यव रामाश्रमं पुण्यजनोपपन्नम्।
गुहेन सार्धं त्वरितो जगाम पुनर्निवेश्यैव चमूं महात्मा।।2.98.18।।
shlokas.translation: Having heard of Rama's hermitage on the Chitrakuta mountain inhabited by pious men, magnanimous Bharata, commanded his army to stay at a distance, and quickly proceeded with Guha. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अयोध्याकाण्डे अष्टनवतितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the ninetyeighth sarga in Ayodhyakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
